strengths: Strong fit for Claude Code-style AI coding
Useful when you need OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible gateway routing
risks: Verify the endpoint is really GLM-5.2 before trusting a gateway
Gateway compatibility and metadata can vary
glm-5.2.verification: Run verify-glm to check tokenizer fingerprint, reasoning_tokens, and optional 1M context support.

risks: Model size and serving cost vary widely
qwen.verification: Use task-specific samples and tokenizer/metadata checks when routed through gateways.

risks: Latency and availability depend on provider/gateway
Reasoning models may increase token usage
deepseek.verification: Validate reasoning behavior, latency, and actual token usage with your own tasks.

strengths: Good fit for research and document-heavy teams
risks: May not be the best first choice for coding-agent workflows
Gateway and context limits should be verified
kimi.verification: Run long-document samples and check whether context truncation occurs.

risks: Availability, data policy, and cost may be constraints for some SMEs
International routing/compliance requirements must be reviewed
claude.verification: Verify policy/compliance fit and cost under realistic usage.

risks: Cost and data governance need review
Gateway substitution risk exists with third-party proxies
gpt.verification: Check provider authenticity, API behavior, and monthly cost under real workloads.

strengths: Best fit when data must stay internal
Full control over deployment and logs
risks: Quality may lag top hosted models for some tasks
local-open-source.verification: Run a small PoC with your own data and measure latency, cost, quality, and ops effort.
